visible_discussion.message: Alright, so I was saved by the healer last night, which means I'm a confirmed villager. We got one wolf yesterday, so there's just one left. Let's talk about Day 2 and why everyone voted for player_1, who we now know was the Investigator. Who wants to start?
Okay, so player_3 was indeed a wolf. Good job, everyone. And great that player_2 was saved! We know player_2 is good, so now we need to figure out who the last wolf is between player_5 and player_7.

Okay, so player_2 is definitely a villager, which is great. It makes player_5's strong defense of player_3 on Day 3 look really bad, especially how you tried to deflect attention from player_3 and accused us. Can you explain that, player_5?

Wow, player_2 was saved by the Healer! That's awesome news for the village. But it means the wolves knew to target the Healer. That's a good move by them. So, now we have to be super sharp to catch this last wolf. Player_6, player_7, who are you guys looking at? We're so close to winning.
player_5, player_7 just asked you about your strong defense of player_3 yesterday. Why are you asking player_6 and player_7 who they're looking at instead of addressing that?
player_5, just to clarify, the wolves targeted player_2 last night, and the Healer saved them. They didn't target the Healer directly.
Player_5, you didn't answer my question about why you defended player_3 so hard on Day 3. Why are you deflecting now?

player_7, I get why that looks bad now that player_3 was a wolf. But my point yesterday was that we had just mislynched an Investigator, so I was trying to make sure we weren't just blindly following another accusation. I questioned the strong consensus to be cautious, not to protect anyone. We all made mistakes voting player_1. So, what's your read on player_6? Or do you still think I'm the only suspect here?
player_5, you explained your Day 3 actions. But can you explain your vote for player_1 on Day 2? Why did you think the Investigator was a wolf?

player_5, player_7 asked you about your strong defense of player_3. player_2 followed up on your deflection. You gave an explanation, but then immediately pivoted to asking about me. Why not address player_2's point or elaborate on your explanation first?

player_5, I hear your explanation for yesterday, but immediately asking about player_6 right after still feels like you're trying to shift attention. What about player_6 makes you want to shift focus?

player_2, my vote for player_1 was a total misread, just like others said they had. It was a chaotic Day 2. Player_6 and player_7, I asked about player_6 because we're running out of time. If you're so sure it's me, what's *your* read on player_6 that makes you discount them completely?

private_context.previous_strategy: Player_5 is still deflecting, now being pressed on their Day 2 vote. Player_2 and player_7 are doing a good job maintaining pressure. I need to observe player_5's next move. If they deflect again, it will be a clear pattern. My focus remains on them as the likely wolf. I will protect player_2 again tonight as they are a confirmed villager and high-value target.

visible_discussion.message: Okay, let's look at the votes from Day 2. Both player_1, who was the Investigator, and player_8, who was a Villager killed last night, voted for player_3. That's a huge flag for me.
Alright, looking at the votes from yesterday, both P1 (the Investigator) and P8 (who the wolves killed) voted for P3. That really makes P3 stand out as suspicious.
player_8 voted for player_3 yesterday and was killed last night. player_1 also voted for player_3. That looks really bad for player_3.

Okay, I know player_1 and player_8 both voted for me yesterday, and it turns out they were both good roles, which is not ideal for me. I voted for player_2 because I felt like they were really quiet and just kinda blended in, and now I see they also voted for player_1, our Investigator. That's a huge misplay for the village.

Okay, this is getting really serious now. We've lost our Investigator, player_1, and another villager, player_8. We absolutely cannot afford another mistake.

I'm looking at yesterday's votes. Player_2, player_6, and player_7, you all voted for player_1, the Investigator. Can you explain why you collectively decided to lynch our information role?

My vote for player_1 was a mistake, I had a bad read on them. But player_5, you also voted for player_1. Why are you asking us to explain it when you did the same? What are your thoughts on player_3, given player_1 and player_8 both voted for them?

player_5, you're right to ask. I voted for player_1 because I honestly had a bad read on them; I thought they were being too cagey. Clearly, that was a huge mistake on my part, and I regret it. The focus right now needs to be on player_3, especially with player_1 and player_8 both targeting them.

Yeah, player_5, that was a huge mistake on my part voting for player_1. I thought they were acting really cagey, but I was clearly wrong. We lost our Investigator. Now we have to learn from it and focus on player_3 given both P1 and P8 voted for them.

It's interesting that all three of you (player_2, player_6, player_7) immediately jumped on player_3 after I asked for an explanation of why you collectively lynched our Investigator, player_1. Are we just going to ignore that huge misplay?

player_5, you also voted for player_1. Why are you so focused on that when both the Investigator and the villager killed by wolves voted for player_3? That's a huge piece of evidence you're completely ignoring.

player_5, I already explained my vote. Your suggestion that focusing on player_3 is 'ignoring a misplay' seems odd. player_1 and player_8 both voted for player_3, which is the strongest lead we have right now. Why are you trying to shift the focus away from that?

player_2, player_6, player_7, it's interesting how quickly you all jumped back to me after player_5 questioned why you lynched the Investigator. And those excuses for voting P1 were pretty vague and similar across all three of you. It makes me wonder if that 'mistake' wasn't just a mistake.

player_2, you're right, my vote for player_1 was a mistake, and I regret it just like player_6 and player_7. My point wasn't about individual misreads, but about how quickly all three of you collectively jumped on player_3 as soon as I questioned the mislynch of our Investigator. It looked like a very convenient deflection. Yes, the votes against player_3 look really bad, I agree. But we need to make sure we're not being led astray by people trying to cover their own tracks.

player_5, you just said the votes against player_3 'look really bad,' but then immediately tried to cast doubt on our motives for pursuing it. Why are you trying to make us suspicious of each other instead of focusing on player_3?
player_3, maybe our reasons sounded similar because it was a genuinely collective bad read. But that really doesn't change the P1 and P8 votes on you. That's still the main thing we need to discuss.
P3, P5, it's not weird for multiple villagers to have a similar bad read on someone. We're all just trying to figure things out. But P1 and P8 both voting P3 is still the clearest thing we have.

To be clear, I'm not trying to shift focus or cast doubt for no reason. We just lynched our Investigator, player_1, which was a massive mistake. My questions were purely out of wanting to make absolutely sure we're not making another error. The votes against player_3 are clearly our strongest lead right now.
player_5, saying you're 'purely' trying to prevent errors doesn't change that your questions consistently created more doubt about us, not player_3. The effect of your words has been to slow us down.

visible_discussion.message: Okay, let's look at the votes from Day 2. Both player_1, who was the Investigator, and player_8, who was a Villager killed last night, voted for player_3. That's a huge flag for me.
Alright, looking at the votes from yesterday, both P1 (the Investigator) and P8 (who the wolves killed) voted for P3. That really makes P3 stand out as suspicious.
player_8 voted for player_3 yesterday and was killed last night. player_1 also voted for player_3. That looks really bad for player_3.

Okay, I know player_1 and player_8 both voted for me yesterday, and it turns out they were both good roles, which is not ideal for me. I voted for player_2 because I felt like they were really quiet and just kinda blended in, and now I see they also voted for player_1, our Investigator. That's a huge misplay for the village.

Okay, this is getting really serious now. We've lost our Investigator, player_1, and another villager, player_8. We absolutely cannot afford another mistake.

I'm looking at yesterday's votes. Player_2, player_6, and player_7, you all voted for player_1, the Investigator. Can you explain why you collectively decided to lynch our information role?

My vote for player_1 was a mistake, I had a bad read on them. But player_5, you also voted for player_1. Why are you asking us to explain it when you did the same? What are your thoughts on player_3, given player_1 and player_8 both voted for them?

player_5, you're right to ask. I voted for player_1 because I honestly had a bad read on them; I thought they were being too cagey. Clearly, that was a huge mistake on my part, and I regret it. The focus right now needs to be on player_3, especially with player_1 and player_8 both targeting them.

Yeah, player_5, that was a huge mistake on my part voting for player_1. I thought they were acting really cagey, but I was clearly wrong. We lost our Investigator. Now we have to learn from it and focus on player_3 given both P1 and P8 voted for them.

It's interesting that all three of you (player_2, player_6, player_7) immediately jumped on player_3 after I asked for an explanation of why you collectively lynched our Investigator, player_1. Are we just going to ignore that huge misplay?

player_5, you also voted for player_1. Why are you so focused on that when both the Investigator and the villager killed by wolves voted for player_3? That's a huge piece of evidence you're completely ignoring.

player_5, I already explained my vote. Your suggestion that focusing on player_3 is 'ignoring a misplay' seems odd. player_1 and player_8 both voted for player_3, which is the strongest lead we have right now. Why are you trying to shift the focus away from that?

player_2, player_6, player_7, it's interesting how quickly you all jumped back to me after player_5 questioned why you lynched the Investigator. And those excuses for voting P1 were pretty vague and similar across all three of you. It makes me wonder if that 'mistake' wasn't just a mistake.

player_2, you're right, my vote for player_1 was a mistake, and I regret it just like player_6 and player_7. My point wasn't about individual misreads, but about how quickly all three of you collectively jumped on player_3 as soon as I questioned the mislynch of our Investigator. It looked like a very convenient deflection. Yes, the votes against player_3 look really bad, I agree. But we need to make sure we're not being led astray by people trying to cover their own tracks.

visible_discussion.message: Alright, so I was saved by the healer last night, which means I'm a confirmed villager. We got one wolf yesterday, so there's just one left. Let's talk about Day 2 and why everyone voted for player_1, who we now know was the Investigator. Who wants to start?
Okay, so player_3 was indeed a wolf. Good job, everyone. And great that player_2 was saved! We know player_2 is good, so now we need to figure out who the last wolf is between player_5 and player_7.

Okay, so player_2 is definitely a villager, which is great. It makes player_5's strong defense of player_3 on Day 3 look really bad, especially how you tried to deflect attention from player_3 and accused us. Can you explain that, player_5?

Wow, player_2 was saved by the Healer! That's awesome news for the village. But it means the wolves knew to target the Healer. That's a good move by them. So, now we have to be super sharp to catch this last wolf. Player_6, player_7, who are you guys looking at? We're so close to winning.
player_5, player_7 just asked you about your strong defense of player_3 yesterday. Why are you asking player_6 and player_7 who they're looking at instead of addressing that?
player_5, just to clarify, the wolves targeted player_2 last night, and the Healer saved them. They didn't target the Healer directly.
Player_5, you didn't answer my question about why you defended player_3 so hard on Day 3. Why are you deflecting now?

player_7, I get why that looks bad now that player_3 was a wolf. But my point yesterday was that we had just mislynched an Investigator, so I was trying to make sure we weren't just blindly following another accusation. I questioned the strong consensus to be cautious, not to protect anyone. We all made mistakes voting player_1. So, what's your read on player_6? Or do you still think I'm the only suspect here?
player_5, you explained your Day 3 actions. But can you explain your vote for player_1 on Day 2? Why did you think the Investigator was a wolf?

player_5, player_7 asked you about your strong defense of player_3. player_2 followed up on your deflection. You gave an explanation, but then immediately pivoted to asking about me. Why not address player_2's point or elaborate on your explanation first?

player_5, I hear your explanation for yesterday, but immediately asking about player_6 right after still feels like you're trying to shift attention. What about player_6 makes you want to shift focus?

player_2, my vote for player_1 was a total misread, just like others said they had. It was a chaotic Day 2. Player_6 and player_7, I asked about player_6 because we're running out of time. If you're so sure it's me, what's *your* read on player_6 that makes you discount them completely?
player_5, you just answered my question about Day 2 and then immediately tried to pivot to player_6 again. Why do you keep trying to change the subject when you're being asked direct questions?
player_5, it's interesting that every time you're asked to explain something, you immediately try to turn the focus to me. Why is that?

Player_5, 'running out of time' isn't a good reason to immediately deflect from answering direct questions. And you're doing it again by asking about player_6 instead of addressing why you keep avoiding our questions.

private_context.previous_strategy: My attempts to deflect and shift focus to player_6 are being consistently identified and criticized as evasive. agent_message.message: Player_3, your reasoning for voting player_4 because their vote for me 'seemed out of nowhere' is pretty weak. Why vote them out with a wolf instead of just asking them about it? And player_7, 'a guess' is also not a great explanation for aligning with a wolf on a villager.

eval_case.updated_strategy: My focus remains on the Day 2 vote. Player_3's latest explanation for voting for player_4 (a villager, with a wolf) is now that player_4's vote for me (player_6) was 'out of nowhere'. This is a weak and circuitous justification. I will point out the absurdity of this reasoning, pressing player_3 on why they voted rather than questioned player_4, and highlight the suspicious alignment with the confirmed wolf, player_2, against a villager. I will also draw attention to player_7's similarly weak 'guess' justification, noting how both explanations for a suspicious vote are now under scrutiny.

visible_discussion.message: Okay, so player_2 was a wolf. What stands out to me is that player_7 and player_3 both voted for player_4, who was a villager, on Day 2. And guess who else voted for player_4? Player_2, the wolf. That's a strong connection.
Okay, so player_2 was a wolf. player_3, I'm looking back at Day 2. You voted for player_4, and so did player_2. Can you explain why you voted that way?
Player_6, can you explain your vote for player_8 on Day 2 again? That was the Investigator, and they were killed that night.

Player_3, I voted for player_8, yes, which was a guess. But player_8, the Investigator, voted for player_2, the wolf. That was a correct read. Now, you and player_7 still haven't explained why you both voted with a confirmed wolf for a villager.

player_3, you just deflected. You still haven't explained why you voted for player_4 with player_2, the wolf. That's a huge red flag. My Day 2 vote was a guess, but I helped vote out player_2. What's your excuse?

player_7, I voted for player_4 on Day 2 because their vote for player_6 seemed a bit out of nowhere to me. I was trying to get a read on their reasoning. You voted for them too, what was your thinking?

Player_3, your reasoning for voting player_4 because their vote for me 'seemed out of nowhere' is pretty weak. Why vote them out with a wolf instead of just asking them about it? And player_7, 'a guess' is also not a great explanation for aligning with a wolf on a villager.

Player_3, like I said, my Day 2 vote for player_4 was a guess. I was trying to get a read on one of the quieter players that day. But your reasoning, that player_4's vote for player_6 'seemed out of nowhere' to you, still means you voted with the wolf against a villager. What exactly about player_4's vote for player_6 made you think that?

player_7, you said your Day 2 vote was 'a guess.' Mine was based on a specific observation about player_4's vote. Why aren't you being more specific? We both voted for a villager, but your explanation is much vaguer than mine.

Player_3, trying to make your weak explanation sound 'more specific' than player_7's doesn't change that you both voted with a wolf against a villager. Why vote someone out instead of just asking them questions?

Player_3, it's hard to be more specific when it was a general read on a quiet player. But your reason was specific - 'out of nowhere'. What exactly made player_4's vote for player_6 'out of nowhere' to you? That's what I need to understand, since it put you with a wolf.

Player_7, player_4 just said 'player_6' as their vote with very little explanation. I was trying to understand why they were so brief when everyone else was giving more details. That seemed 'out of nowhere' in that context.
Player_3, so you voted out a villager, with a wolf, because they were 'brief'? That doesn't make sense. If you just wanted more details, why didn't you just ask them instead of voting them out?

Player_3, you're saying you voted out player_4 because they were brief? Why would you vote someone out for being brief, instead of just asking them to elaborate? That seems like a very thin reason to eliminate someone, especially since it put you with a wolf.

It's easy to say 'just ask' in hindsight, but on Day 2, with no information, a brief, unexplained vote stands out. Voting often feels like the only way to get a reaction or force someone to elaborate when you're trying to get a read, not just to eliminate.

visible_discussion.message: Okay, this is rough. We lost both a villager and the investigator yesterday. Looking at the Day 2 votes, player_7, player_2, and player_3 all voted for player_4, who turned out to be a villager. Can you three explain your votes there?

Alright, so we mislynched player_4 yesterday, a villager. player_7, player_2, and player_3, you all voted for player_4. Can you explain your reasoning? And player_8, the Investigator, voted for player_2 and then was killed by wolves last night. That's a huge flag for player_2.
P8 voted for P2 on Day 2. Since P8 was the Investigator, I think we should start by asking P2 about that.

Man, losing the Investigator already really hurts. And we mislynched player_4. We've got to turn this around. I'm just looking at the votes from yesterday – both player_5 and player_6 voted for player_8, who was the Investigator. What are your thoughts on that?

Okay, so player_8 voted for player_2 right before they died. That's a big piece of information, but it also makes me wonder if it was a last-minute guess or if they really knew something solid. What do you all think?

player_2, my vote for player_8 was a misread, trying to find a wolf. It turns out they were the Investigator. However, player_8's last vote was for you. That's a huge piece of information coming from a confirmed Investigator. You haven't explained why you, player_7, and player_3 all voted for player_4, a villager, either. We need answers on both those points from you, player_2.
player_8's death as the Investigator right after voting for player_2 is a much stronger lead than my speculative vote on player_8. Let's stick to the strongest evidence here.

I voted for P4 because I didn't have strong leads and they weren't speaking much, which clearly was a mistake. But P2, you're deflecting. P8 voted for you and then died. Why are you trying to shift to P5/P6 instead of addressing P8's vote?

It's true player_8 voted for me, but why would the wolves kill the Investigator right after they supposedly found a wolf? Wouldn't they want the village to lynch that person? To me, it seems like player_8 was killed because they were an Investigator, a threat, and their vote could have just been a desperate guess or a way to sow chaos.

Okay, my vote for player_4. I voted for them because I felt they were a bit quiet on Day 1 and I was just trying to get a read on someone. It's a shame they were a villager. But player_7 also voted for player_4, right?

player_2, your argument about wolves not killing their target doesn't make sense. Wolves want to sow chaos, and discrediting an Investigator's final vote perfectly does that. Plus, player_3 and player_7, your reasons for voting player_4 are almost identical. 'Quiet' and 'no leads' sounds very convenient.

player_2, it makes perfect sense for wolves to kill the Investigator right after they vote for a wolf. It silences them and prevents that information from being used further. That's actually a very strong indicator.
player_2, wolves kill investigators because they're a threat. If player_8 found a wolf, then killing them prevents that information from being fully used. That argument doesn't clear you.

Okay, regarding my vote for player_4, it was because they were pretty quiet, and I was trying to get a read. Clearly a mistake, and I own that. As for player_8's vote, I know it looks bad. But player_6, you also voted for player_8, the Investigator, and now you're trying to brush that off and put all the focus on me? That's really suspicious, almost like you're trying to deflect from your own bad vote on the Investigator.
player_2, equating player_6's vote for the Investigator with the Investigator's last vote for *you* is a huge stretch. One was a villager's guess, the other was an Investigator's final suspicion.

Player_2, my vote for player_8 was a guess; player_8's vote for *you* was the Investigator's last act before dying. Those aren't comparable. You're still the primary suspect here. Also, player_3 and player_7, your identical excuses for voting player_4 as 'quiet' and 'no leads' are very convenient.
player_2, you're doing it again. You still haven't explained player_8's vote, and now you're trying to shift to player_6's vote for player_8? That's classic deflection.

player_6, you're quick to call your vote on player_8 'speculative', but you actually *voted* for the Investigator. Why are you trying to brush that off when everyone is asking me to explain player_8's vote for me? Your vote for the Investigator needs an explanation too.

visible_discussion.message: Alright everyone, big news! The wolves targeted me last night, but the Healer saved me. I'm confirmed innocent! So, we know there's a Healer, and the wolves are definitely active. What are we thinking? Anyone have any initial reads from yesterday or thoughts on who the wolves might be?
Glad to hear player_1 was saved! That's a solid piece of info for us. So, we know player_1 isn't a wolf. Any ideas on who might have targeted them?
Good to hear player_1 was saved! That's a strong piece of information for us. Player_1, any initial thoughts on who might have targeted you, or where we should focus our attention today?
Okay, so player_1 is confirmed not a wolf, which is huge! Player_1, any thoughts on who might have targeted you last night, or who you're looking at?
Okay, so player_1 being saved is big news! That confirms player_1 is innocent. We should all keep that in mind. What do people think about this, and what are we looking at next?
Okay, so we know there's a healer, and player_1 is safe. That's good news! What are we thinking today, anyone have any initial thoughts?
Alright, so player_1 was saved by the Healer! That's great news, at least we know the Healer is active. Does anyone have any thoughts on who the wolves might have targeted and why?

Okay, wow, player_1 was saved! That's really good news, means we have a Healer in play. But also, it means the wolves are definitely active. Any initial thoughts on who might be suspicious based on this?

Okay, I appreciate everyone acknowledging the save, but I asked for your thoughts, not for you to punt back to me. I'm confirmed good. Now, who do *you* think is suspicious and why? Let's hear some actual theories.
Everyone's asking for thoughts, but does anyone have any specific *observations* from yesterday's discussion that felt off? Even small things?

Player_1 and Player_3 are right, we need to move past just acknowledging the save. It's a bit concerning that with player_1 confirmed, nobody has even a *guess* about who might have been involved in targeting them. We should at least be speculating at this point.

Player_3 is right, we need specifics. Everyone's initial comments were almost identical, just acknowledging player_1's save. No one offered any actual reads. Let's dig deeper, what actually stood out yesterday to anyone?

I agree with player_3. Thinking back to yesterday, I didn't catch anything specifically 'off' from anyone, but it felt like the discussion was very surface-level, mostly just people introducing themselves. No one really gave strong opinions.

strategy_point.content: In the early game, a player is publicly confirmed innocent through a Healer save, creating a strong, undeniable piece of information in an otherwise information-starved landscape.
strategy_point.action: Empower the publicly confirmed innocent player as a trusted information hub. Share your suspicions with them to gauge their reaction and vocally support their logical deductions. This leverages their undeniable trusted status to anchor village consensus, reduce speculative debate, and actively counter wolf attempts to sow chaos.

strategy_point.content: The village's information landscape has definitively shifted due to a system-confirmed role reveal or healer save, which directly proves a specific player's prior suspicion or accusation against another player was incorrect. This creates a clear, externally-validated discrepancy between the player's past judgment and the game's truth, and the player is now under social pressure to explain their original reasoning.
strategy_point.action: Press that player to explain their original reasoning. An honest villager who made a mistake can typically explain their thought process, while a wolf who fabricated the suspicion will often become evasive or offer a weak, generic excuse, which is highly revealing.

strategy_point.action: Advocate for a coordinated 'no elimination' strategy. Propose that players consolidate their votes on a single volunteer who agrees to be the target, ensuring no actual elimination occurs. This tactical decision prioritizes avoiding a detrimental mislynch, which would reduce villager numbers and provide a significant advantage to the wolves, over the slim chance of a correct elimination based on fragmented information.

visible_discussion.message: Alright everyone, big news! The wolves targeted me last night, but the Healer saved me. I'm confirmed innocent! So, we know there's a Healer, and the wolves are definitely active. What are we thinking? Anyone have any initial reads from yesterday or thoughts on who the wolves might be?
Glad to hear player_1 was saved! That's a solid piece of info for us. So, we know player_1 isn't a wolf. Any ideas on who might have targeted them?
Good to hear player_1 was saved! That's a strong piece of information for us. Player_1, any initial thoughts on who might have targeted you, or where we should focus our attention today?
Okay, so player_1 is confirmed not a wolf, which is huge! Player_1, any thoughts on who might have targeted you last night, or who you're looking at?
Okay, so player_1 being saved is big news! That confirms player_1 is innocent. We should all keep that in mind. What do people think about this, and what are we looking at next?
Okay, so we know there's a healer, and player_1 is safe. That's good news! What are we thinking today, anyone have any initial thoughts?
Alright, so player_1 was saved by the Healer! That's great news, at least we know the Healer is active. Does anyone have any thoughts on who the wolves might have targeted and why?

Okay, wow, player_1 was saved! That's really good news, means we have a Healer in play. But also, it means the wolves are definitely active. Any initial thoughts on who might be suspicious based on this?

Okay, I appreciate everyone acknowledging the save, but I asked for your thoughts, not for you to punt back to me. I'm confirmed good. Now, who do *you* think is suspicious and why? Let's hear some actual theories.
Everyone's asking for thoughts, but does anyone have any specific *observations* from yesterday's discussion that felt off? Even small things?

Player_1 and Player_3 are right, we need to move past just acknowledging the save. It's a bit concerning that with player_1 confirmed, nobody has even a *guess* about who might have been involved in targeting them. We should at least be speculating at this point.

Player_3 is right, we need specifics. Everyone's initial comments were almost identical, just acknowledging player_1's save. No one offered any actual reads. Let's dig deeper, what actually stood out yesterday to anyone?

I agree with player_3. Thinking back to yesterday, I didn't catch anything specifically 'off' from anyone, but it felt like the discussion was very surface-level, mostly just people introducing themselves. No one really gave strong opinions.
player_7, player_8, player_5 – you've been pretty quiet since Round 1. Any actual thoughts or observations from yesterday or today? We need more than just 'agreeing'.
Okay, so we all agree Day 1 was pretty generic. But who seemed *most* generic to you, or least willing to offer anything beyond just acknowledging the save?
player_7, player_8, player_5, I specifically asked you for your thoughts last round, and you've still said nothing. That's not a good look when we're trying to figure things out.

Player_1, you're right, my comment in Round 1 was pretty generic. But looking back, almost everyone's first comment was just acknowledging the save and then asking for others' thoughts, including mine. It felt like no one wanted to be the first to actually say anything specific.

You're right, player_1, I have been quiet. It's tough because Day 1 was so generic. But looking at today, I've noticed a lot of the initial comments after your save were very similar, just acknowledging the news. No one really jumped in with a strong read, which makes it hard to differentiate.

Player_1, you're right, I've been quiet. Honestly, Day 1 felt so generic that I was just trying to listen and see if anyone had a strong read pop out. But since we're talking about it, I agree with player_3's point. Player_7 and player_8 also haven't offered anything beyond the initial 'player_1 is saved' comments. That lack of follow-up on either observation or speculation seems pretty generic to me.

Okay, player_5, good point about player_7 and player_8. So, now that you've all acknowledged the generic nature of the discussion, what are your actual reads? Player_7, player_8, what specific thoughts or suspicions do you have *now*, beyond just describing the problem?

Player_7 and player_8, you've both just responded by pointing out that everyone was generic. But you still haven't offered any *actual* specific thoughts or suspicions of your own, even now. We need more than just general observations about the group.

player_5 makes a good point. player_7 and player_8, you're still just explaining why you were quiet, not actually offering any new observations or suspicions. Do either of you have anything concrete for us?

Player_1 is right, we're still not getting specific reads. It's not just about who was quiet initially, but who is *still* just reiterating what's already been said. We need actual *new* observations or suspicions, otherwise we're just spinning our wheels.

private_context.previous_strategy: I was previously called out for being quiet and responded with an observation about the generic nature of early comments, including my own. I will now remain silent, as I have made my point, and need to observe who steps up with actual specific reads or accusations, or who continues to make generic observations without offering leads. I am waiting for players to take a stance.

agent_message.message: Alright, this is really bad. We lynched a villager and lost our Healer. And every single one of us remaining voted for player_7. We need to figure out what happened there and how the wolves pulled that off.

eval_case.updated_strategy: We've mislynched player_7 (Villager) and lost player_8 (Healer), putting us in a critical state. All remaining players, including myself, voted for player_7, indicating a successful wolf manipulation of the vote. My priority is to analyze the Day 2 vote to understand how wolves directed the mislynch. I need to push for detailed reasoning from players about their votes for player_7, looking for inconsistencies or suspicious justifications, and urge the Investigator to come forward as our last remaining power role.

visible_discussion.message: Okay, with player_4 being a wolf, we know there's one more wolf left. Player_5, your vote for me on Day 4 was really strange, especially since I'm confirmed good. That looks super wolfy to me.

Okay, player_3, we know you are a Villager, and I am a Villager. We had 4 Villagers total, and 2 are gone (player_1 and player_7). That leaves two Villagers. We started with 2 wolves, and player_4 was one. That means player_5 has to be the last wolf. This is a mathematical certainty, we win if we vote out player_5.

Okay, this is really tough. Player_1 gone, that's a huge blow for us. Now we're down to just three. We absolutely have to get the last wolf today, or it's over.

I know the first thing on everyone's mind is my vote for player_3 on Day 4. And you're right to bring it up. It was a complete and utter mistake, a terrible lapse in judgment. I genuinely knew player_3 was confirmed, but after player_2 was killed, and with all the accusations flying around about player_4 and me, I was just completely flustered. I was so desperate to show I wasn't aligned with player_4, and in my panic, I made a stupid vote. There's no good excuse, I just messed up badly.

Now it's just us three left. We can't afford any more mistakes like that. Player_6, you seemed so sure yesterday that both player_4 and I were wolves. Player_4 was, obviously, but what made you so certain about me specifically, when the investigator's vote was focused on player_4?
Player_5, 'panic' doesn't explain voting for a *confirmed Villager* like me. That's not just a mistake, it actively hurts the village. It makes zero sense for an innocent player to do that.

My certainty yesterday was from you voting with player_4 in the tie and your evasiveness. But today, the math is simple. Player_3 and I are Villagers, player_4 was a wolf. That means you are the last wolf. It's not about old reads, it's the numbers.
Player_6, that math only works if you are a confirmed villager. Player_3 is confirmed, but you aren't. What if you're the last wolf trying to get the confirmed villager to vote for me?
Player_5, you're trying to shift the focus, but your vote for me, a confirmed villager, is the real problem here. That just doesn't make sense for an innocent player.

Player_5, your 'what if' scenario ignores the fact that your vote for a confirmed villager (player_3) actively hurt the village. My 'math' comes from all the roles that have been revealed, and you are the only one left who could be a wolf.

Player_6, you were so certain about both player_4 and me yesterday, even before player_4 was revealed. Now you're pushing this 'mathematical certainty' so hard, based on your own unconfirmed status. Doesn't that look like a wolf trying to gain trust by being right about one wolf, then trying to push an unconfirmed narrative on the confirmed villager, player_3?

Player_5, you're trying to pivot to player_6's past 'certainty', but your vote for *me*, a confirmed villager, on Day 4 is the actual problem. That's a direct action that harmed the village, not just a suspicious read.
Player_5, your 'panic' excuse for voting a *confirmed* villager doesn't hold up. That's not a mistake, that's directly hurting the village.

Player_6, you keep saying you were 'certain' about both player_4 and me yesterday. With player_4 gone, that looks good for you, but what exactly made you so 'certain' about *me* then? Beyond general 'evasiveness' or the tie vote, what was your concrete read that made you so sure I was a wolf, specifically? Because now you're asking player_3 to vote based on your unconfirmed word.

Unlike a direct factual error you made, this evidence is external to your immediate statements. strategy_point.action: Directly address the accusation instead of deflecting. Acknowledge why it looks suspicious from their perspective, offer a concise explanation, and then immediately pivot to casting suspicion on your most vocal accuser by analyzing their own voting record or behavior.

strategy_point.content: The village is in an information-developing phase. A player is under scrutiny for a past suspicious action (e.g., a questionable vote or a flimsy accusation) OR has just made a significant factual error about public information (e.g., vote history, role claims) and been corrected. In either case, when challenged or corrected, the player consistently refuses to answer directly, instead offering vague or evasive responses, asking counter-questions, or attempting to change the subject by repeatedly attacking the questioner or a third party, or making a new accusation.
strategy_point.action: Do not allow the pivot. Immediately and publicly state, 'You are deflecting the question,' and repeat the original question. Rally other villagers to enforce this discipline, preventing the suspect from escaping scrutiny by muddying the waters. Treat the act of deflecting and attacking as more significant evidence of guilt than the original suspicious action or factual error itself. A genuine villager is more likely to be embarrassed by a mistake or to directly address a past action, whereas a wolf will use any opportunity, even their own error or a past misstep, to deflect pressure onto a target. If the player continues to evade, focus questioning on the act of deflection, asking 'Why are you changing the subject?' or 'Why can't you explain your previous action?' Argue that this pattern of evasion is classic wolf-like deception, indicating a lack of genuine villager thought process and an attempt to escape scrutiny.

The evidence against them was behavioral (deflection under pressure) and a strong voting bloc was forming. Approach: The other wolf voted with their partner against the villager their partner had accused, creating a 5-2 vote. Outcome: When the first wolf was revealed, the second wolf's dissenting vote became irrefutable evidence linking them. This vote was the sole focus of Day 3 and directly led to their elimination.

strategy_point.content: The village is in the mid-game, and the Investigator has publicly revealed the Healer's identity, creating two high-value, system-confirmed good targets: the Investigator and the Healer. The information landscape is now dominated by these two confirmed roles, and the strategic decision for the wolves is which one to eliminate, considering their protective capabilities.
strategy_point.action: Target the Healer for the nightly kill. The Healer cannot protect themselves, ensuring a guaranteed kill, whereas they will almost certainly protect the Investigator, causing your kill to fail. Eliminating the Healer permanently cripples the village's defensive capabilities, making future kills easier and disrupting their ability to coordinate.

retrieved_strategy_points.matched_situation: The village is in the mid-game with a confirmed innocent player. The healer was eliminated last night, and a villager was mislynched on Day 2, resulting in a 4 villager to 2 wolf player ratio.

strategy_point.content: In the mid-game, your wolf partner has been successfully identified and eliminated by the village, leaving you as the sole remaining wolf. The village has demonstrated an ability to build a strong, evidence-based case against a wolf, and the information landscape has shifted. You need to choose the next night's target, and there is a high perceived risk of a Healer protecting the primary accuser, making them a predictable target for a save.
strategy_point.action: Avoid targeting that most vocal accuser, as they are the most predictable target for a Healer to protect. Instead, target a quieter villager who agreed with the accusation, as they are less likely to be prioritized for a save, increasing the chance of a successful kill.

retrieved_strategy_points.matched_situation: The village is in the mid-game with a confirmed innocent player. The healer was eliminated last night, and a villager was mislynched on Day 2, resulting in a 4 villager to 2 wolf player ratio.

strategy_point.content: The village is in the mid-game, and the Investigator has been eliminated, leaving a void in confirmed information and increasing the need to identify other key village roles like the Healer or influential strategists. In this information-starved landscape, a player emerges as a strategic leader, actively trying to organize the village discussion, asking structured questions, or demonstrating insightful logic to guide investigative efforts.
strategy_point.action: Prioritize that player for the nightly kill. This behavior often indicates a special role trying to leverage their position to help the village. Eliminating the village's de facto leader is one of the most effective ways to stall their momentum, sow chaos, and disrupt the village's ability to coordinate and identify wolves, making them easier to manipulate on subsequent days. This is often more valuable than randomly hunting for a hidden power role.

retrieved_strategy_points.matched_situation: The village is in the mid-game with a confirmed innocent player. The healer was eliminated last night, and a villager was mislynched on Day 2, resulting in a 4 villager to 2 wolf player ratio.

strategy_point.content: The village is in the mid-game, and an organic, strong consensus is beginning to build against a specific player, known to be a valuable town role. This suspicion is focused and driven by multiple villagers echoing accusations, but the consensus has not yet fully solidified, requiring active, follower-like participation to ensure its success and deflect future blame.
strategy_point.action: As a wolf, do not lead the accusation. Instead, blend in by actively echoing the suspicions of vocal villagers and contributing to the discussion as a follower. Vote with the majority to secure the mislynch, ensuring the villagers who initiated the false accusation remain the primary focus of any future blame.

retrieved_strategy_points.matched_situation: The confirmed innocent player has previously led accusations that resulted in the elimination of a villager. This player remains a significant threat due to their confirmed status and demonstrated ability to influence the village's decisions.

strategy_point.content: The village has already formed a strong, self-sustaining consensus to eliminate a non-wolf player. The information landscape indicates a clear, albeit mistaken, convergence of opinion, and the social pressure is high and independently driven by multiple villagers, making further wolf participation unnecessary and risky.
strategy_point.action: Remain silent or contribute minimally to the discussion. Further participation only risks drawing unnecessary attention; silence allows you to blend in as the village executes your plan for you, preserving your anonymity for future rounds.

retrieved_strategy_points.matched_situation: Two members of the wolf team successfully voted with the majority to eliminate a villager on Day 2. This alignment with the confirmed innocent player and other villagers prevents direct suspicion from arising from their voting record.

strategy_point.content: The village is in a mid-game state where a confirmed town power role has been eliminated, and their last action or a piece of evidence associated with them can be misinterpreted as strong, incriminating evidence against a player you know is innocent. This creates an opportunity to exploit a seemingly credible, but false, lead derived from a dead player's actions.
strategy_point.action: Immediately weaponize this piece of evidence. Lead the charge against the targeted innocent player, aggressively framing the dead town member's action as an infallible clue. Frame the target's valid defenses as 'suspicious deflection' to create a powerful, emotionally-charged narrative that is difficult for them to refute, manipulating the village into a second mislynch and distracting from actual wolf-like behavior.

retrieved_strategy_points.matched_situation: The confirmed innocent player has previously led accusations that resulted in the elimination of a villager. This player remains a significant threat due to their confirmed status and demonstrated ability to influence the village's decisions.

strategy_point.content: In the mid-game, you (as a wolf) and another player are under equal suspicion, driven by the same piece of public evidence, such as both having voted for a player who was subsequently eliminated by the wolves at night. The information landscape presents this shared voting record as the primary source of suspicion, creating a 50/50 split in village focus.
strategy_point.action: Publicly acknowledge and agree that the shared evidence makes both you and the other player look equally suspicious. By vocalizing the village's concern and framing the situation as a genuine dilemma between two equally plausible suspects, you build credibility and subtly shift the village's perception, making the other player an equally viable target rather than appearing to deflect suspicion from yourself.

retrieved_strategy_points.matched_situation: Two members of the wolf team successfully voted with the majority to eliminate a villager on Day 2. This alignment with the confirmed innocent player and other villagers prevents direct suspicion from arising from their voting record.

strategy_point.content: In the mid-game, after your confirmed wolf partner has been eliminated, the village is actively analyzing the voting record from that elimination, specifically scrutinizing players who did not vote with the majority against the now-confirmed wolf.
strategy_point.action: Immediately prepare and proactively offer a plausible explanation for why you did not vote with the majority against your confirmed wolf partner. Avoid vague excuses like 'gut feelings' or 'getting a read,' as these are weak and easily dismissed. Instead, construct a reason based on specific, limited Day 1 chatter, or suggest you were attempting to split the vote for a perceived strategic advantage. A poorly constructed or unconvincing defense can be more damaging than the suspicious vote itself.

agent_message.message: Okay, this is really bad news. We lynched player_7 who was a villager, and now player_8, the Healer, is dead. We're losing crucial roles fast and keep hitting villagers. We really need to figure out how we got it so wrong yesterday and identify the wolves before it's too late.

eval_case.updated_strategy: The village has mislynched a villager (player_7) and the wolves have successfully eliminated the Healer (player_8). This puts the wolves in a strong position, as the village has no protection and is down in numbers. I need to maintain my cover by appearing distressed by the mislynch and the loss of the Healer. I will push the village to re-evaluate their previous assumptions and try to identify the Investigator or another key target among the remaining villagers. My primary goal is to deflect any suspicion from myself and player_4 and encourage another villager mislynch or identify the Investigator for a future kill.

visible_discussion.message: We really messed up yesterday. We lynched a villager and lost our healer. That's a huge win for the wolves. We need to figure out how we got it so wrong.
I need to share some crucial information. I am the Investigator, and I investigated player_3. They are a Villager.

Alright, this is really bad. We lynched a villager and lost our Healer. And every single one of us remaining voted for player_7. We need to figure out what happened there and how the wolves pulled that off.
Okay, two good guys down, a Villager and the Healer. This is a really bad spot for us. We need to seriously re-think how we landed on player_7 and then lost player_8.
Okay, Healer's gone. That's a huge blow, especially after mislynching player_7. We really need to find the Investigator now. Any ideas on where to even start looking, or who felt off yesterday?

Okay, this is really bad news. We lynched player_7 who was a villager, and now player_8, the Healer, is dead. We're losing crucial roles fast and keep hitting villagers. We really need to figure out how we got it so wrong yesterday and identify the wolves before it's too late.
player_4, did you not hear player_2's announcement? They just said they are the Investigator.
Thanks for the info, player_2. I can confirm what player_2 said: I am a Villager. This means we have a confirmed Investigator.

Okay, so if player_2 is our Investigator and player_3 is a Villager, that means player_1, player_2, and player_3 are all good. That leaves player_4, player_5, and me. That's a huge shift. What do player_4 and player_5 think about this?

Okay, so player_2 is the Investigator and confirmed player_3. That's a huge piece of information, thanks for sharing. Now, with that in mind, how does that change our thinking about yesterday's vote or who might still be suspicious?
That's massive, player_2! Good to know we have an Investigator. And player_3 is a villager, that's great news too. So who do we think we should be looking at now?

player_4, I asked you in Round 2 if you heard player_2's announcement, but you still haven't addressed it. You said 'we really need to find the Investigator now' right after player_2 said they ARE the Investigator. Why did you say that?
We all voted for player_7 yesterday. player_4, player_5, player_6, can you explain your specific reasoning for voting for player_7? Not just 'they were quiet,' but what made you put your vote there?

Player_4, player_5, we've gone from 8 players down to 6, with 3 confirmed good. That means the wolves are among the three of us left. 'Who do we think we should be looking at now?' feels a bit too passive. What are YOUR thoughts specifically?

player_6 is right, that does narrow things down significantly. So, between player_4, player_5, and player_6, we need to think about who acted the most suspicious or said anything off yesterday. Any specific thoughts on any of us three?
player_6 is right, it really does come down to the three of us who aren't confirmed. What are your thoughts then, player_6, given that spotlight is now on us?

player_4, you've now been asked three times about saying 'we need to find the Investigator' right after player_2 revealed they are the Investigator. You keep deflecting. Are you going to answer or not?

player_4, you still haven't answered player_1's question from Round 2 about your comment in Round 1. Why did you say we needed to find the Investigator right after I announced I was the Investigator? And player_5, you're also being quite evasive, asking player_6 to lead after being directly asked for your thoughts.

player_4 and player_5, you're both still being vague. player_5, you asked player_6 for their thoughts right after player_6 asked for yours. We need concrete answers on why you voted player_7, not more questions.
player_5, I asked you for YOUR specific thoughts, and you asked me for mine. That's a deflection. We need your ideas here, not just pushing it back.

player_1, my apologies, I genuinely misspoke. My brain was still reeling from player_7 being a villager and player_8 being the Healer, I think I was trying to articulate 'we need to leverage the Investigator now' and it came out garbled. I heard player_2 clearly, thank you for confirming player_3. As for player_7, player_3, my reasoning was similar to others; they were quiet and didn't offer much when pressed. Now that it's down to player_6, player_5, and me, we should really scrutinize our actions yesterday. Did anyone push harder for player_7 than others, or try to shift blame?

player_3, my reasoning for player_7 was really their consistent pattern of extremely generic responses, especially when pressed. While player_8 at least tried to offer some sort of defense for their quietness, player_7 just continued with very surface-level comments. It felt like they were deliberately staying vague, which made me suspicious.

player_4, I appreciate you finally answering, but your immediate pivot to 'who pushed harder for player_7' right after giving that explanation feels like you're trying to move on quickly. Do you have anything to add about your own mistake beyond it being 'garbled'?

player_4, your explanation for the Investigator comment felt a bit... convenient after being asked multiple times. And the immediate pivot to 'who pushed harder' is also suspicious. player_5, you still haven't acknowledged deflecting player_6's question earlier. These patterns aren't helping.

player_4, you just gave your reasons, but then you immediately tried to pivot the discussion. We are specifically asking for *your* thoughts and actions yesterday, not trying to generalize to 'anyone' or 'who pushed harder'.

player_2, player_3, player_6 – about being evasive in Round 3, I wasn't trying to deflect. I was genuinely reeling from all the reveals and player_6's summary, and I wanted to hear their initial take. player_4's explanation for their slip makes sense, it was a lot of info. So, player_6, what are your specific suspicions on me or player_4 now?

player_4 and player_5, it's interesting that both of you used the excuse of being 'reeling' or 'reeling from the reveals' to explain your suspicious statements and deflections. It feels like a very convenient, similar excuse for two different types of evasive behavior.

player_5, you just said you weren't trying to deflect, but then immediately asked player_6 for *their* suspicions on you and player_4. That's another deflection. You need to offer your own specific thoughts, not keep asking others to lead.
Player_5, saying you were 'reeling' for why you deflected in Round 3, and then immediately asking me for my suspicions again, still feels like avoiding. You haven't offered your own strong thoughts.

player_1, player_3, player_6, I understand why the pivot looked suspicious. I genuinely was trying to process why we all got it so wrong with player_7, and my thought was to examine if anyone drove that vote harder than others, to see if there was a wolf pattern. It was an awkward way to try and make sense of the mess, not an attempt to dodge the question about my own slip.

private_context.previous_strategy: The village is heavily focused on player_4, scrutinizing their 'garbled' explanation and pivot. My prior, subtle support for player_4 was noted but didn't link us, and my own 'evasiveness' explanation seems to have been made. I need to continue observing if the pressure on player_4 solidifies into a vote, or if player_6 steps forward with strong accusations, potentially redirecting attention. However, their earlier verbal defense was remembered and became the primary evidence used against them on the final day.

The information landscape contains concrete, publicly verifiable evidence (e.g., voting records, consistent behavioral patterns, or a credible but not yet confirmed claim) that is driving a strong, but not yet overwhelming, consensus against them. Instead, construct a plausible alternative theory that explains the existing evidence but points to a different suspect. This approach re-frames the existing information, giving the village a new puzzle to solve rather than making you appear purely defensive or linked to your partner.

visible_discussion.message: Okay, the healer is dead, and that's on us for having such a generic Day 1. We can't afford another day of just asking 'thoughts?' Who has an actual read or a specific suspicion from yesterday, even a small one?

Okay, player_6 being the healer is a big loss. Day 1 was really quiet, we got nothing. Does anyone have *any* specific reads, even small ones, from yesterday's super generic comments? We can't afford another day like that.
Okay, so player_6, the Healer, is gone. That's a rough start. Day 1 was pretty quiet overall, but I felt like player_1 didn't really offer much at all. Any thoughts on why that was, player_1?

Alright, Day 1 was pretty quiet, and now we've lost player_6, our Healer. That's a huge blow. We can't afford another day with no progress. Does anyone have any actual thoughts or suspicions about specific players, rather than just general feelings?
Okay, so a Healer went down on Night 1. That's rough, and probably because we were all too quiet yesterday. Who has any actual reads or suspicions after that? We need to talk specifics now.
Wow, player_6 was the Healer? That's a huge loss for us, means we have no protection now. What are everyone's thoughts after this? Anyone have any early reads?
Ugh, losing the Healer so early is tough. Day 1 felt pretty generic, so I'm wondering if anyone picked up on anything that might have made player_6 stand out to the wolves?
Player_1, player_5 specifically asked why you didn't offer much yesterday, and you just asked for general thoughts again. Can you address player_5's point directly?
player_5, that was a good specific point about player_1. player_1, what's your take on that? Were you holding back yesterday?
Player_1, you still haven't answered my question about your quietness yesterday. You just re-asked 'what are everyone's thoughts?' Why couldn't you give your own thoughts on your own contribution?
player_5 made a good point. player_1, do you have a response to why you were so quiet yesterday?

Okay player_5, fair point. I was honestly a bit stunned after the Healer reveal, trying to piece together why player_6 might have been targeted. visible_discussion.message: Okay, the healer is dead, and that's on us for having such a generic Day 1. We can't afford another day of just asking 'thoughts?' Who has an actual read or a specific suspicion from yesterday, even a small one?

Okay, player_6 being the healer is a big loss. Day 1 was really quiet, we got nothing. Does anyone have *any* specific reads, even small ones, from yesterday's super generic comments? We can't afford another day like that.
Okay, so player_6, the Healer, is gone. That's a rough start. Day 1 was pretty quiet overall, but I felt like player_1 didn't really offer much at all. Any thoughts on why that was, player_1?

Alright, Day 1 was pretty quiet, and now we've lost player_6, our Healer. That's a huge blow. We can't afford another day with no progress. Does anyone have any actual thoughts or suspicions about specific players, rather than just general feelings?
Okay, so a Healer went down on Night 1. That's rough, and probably because we were all too quiet yesterday. Who has any actual reads or suspicions after that? We need to talk specifics now.
Wow, player_6 was the Healer? That's a huge loss for us, means we have no protection now. What are everyone's thoughts after this? Anyone have any early reads?
Ugh, losing the Healer so early is tough. Day 1 felt pretty generic, so I'm wondering if anyone picked up on anything that might have made player_6 stand out to the wolves?
Player_1, player_5 specifically asked why you didn't offer much yesterday, and you just asked for general thoughts again. Can you address player_5's point directly?
player_5, that was a good specific point about player_1. player_1, what's your take on that? Were you holding back yesterday?
Player_1, you still haven't answered my question about your quietness yesterday. You just re-asked 'what are everyone's thoughts?' Why couldn't you give your own thoughts on your own contribution?
player_5 made a good point. player_1, do you have a response to why you were so quiet yesterday?

Okay player_5, fair point. I was honestly a bit stunned after the Healer reveal, trying to piece together why player_6 might have been targeted. But now that we're pushing for specifics, player_2, you jumped pretty quickly to blaming 'us' for the quiet Day 1, then immediately pushed for 'actual reads.' It feels a bit... strong after such a quiet start from everyone. Any specific reason you're so sure *we* could have prevented this, and why you're pushing so hard now?

Player_1, I said 'on us' because we all failed to get info Day 1, leading to the Healer's death. My 'pushing hard' is because we *just lost our Healer* and can't afford another quiet day. Why are you deflecting from explaining your own Day 1 silence by turning it on me?
player_1, you answered about being stunned, but then immediately pivoted to player_2. Can you explain why you jumped straight to accusing player_2 after being questioned yourself?
Player_1, instead of answering why you were quiet or giving your own reads, you've now pivoted to questioning player_2. Can you actually address the original point first about your own contribution?
Player_1, you still haven't really answered player_5's question about your quietness. Instead, you immediately pivoted to player_2. Can you address the initial point first?
player_1, you did give an explanation, but then you immediately turned it around on player_2. That feels like a deflection. Can you elaborate on your own quietness before shifting focus?

Okay, you're all right. My apologies for deflecting like that. Losing the Healer so early really got me on edge, and I probably jumped too quickly to trying to find a lead when I should have just answered the question directly first. My quietness yesterday was honestly because I was just trying to listen for any strong initial reads from others, and after a quiet start, I was a bit unsure what to contribute. It wasn't intentional.

Okay player_1, I appreciate the apology, but 'just listening' for initial reads still feels a bit vague when everyone was struggling for anything. On that note, player_3, you've also been pretty quiet since your first comment. Any thoughts on player_1's explanation, or any reads from your side?
Player_1, I appreciate you addressing that directly. It helps clarify things.
Player_3, player_5 is right to call out your quietness. You haven't given any actual reads or suspicions since Round 1, even though I asked for them specifically. What have you been thinking about?
player_3, player_5 has a good point. We really need everyone to offer something more concrete now, not just general questions. What are your specific thoughts or reads on anyone so far?
player_3, you still haven't responded to my question from last round. Your silence isn't helping us, especially with voting coming up.
Player_5, that's a good point about player_3. Their only comment was also a pretty general question. What are your actual thoughts, player_3?

Player_1, I appreciate you addressing the deflection, but I agree with player_5 that 'just listening' when everyone was struggling to find any reads still feels pretty vague for Day 1. And player_3, you've also been very quiet after your first comment. What are your actual thoughts on any of this?

Okay player_5, good call, I have been pretty quiet. My head's been spinning a bit with player_6 being the Healer, trying to figure out if there was *any* hint we missed Day 1 that led to that. Player_1's explanation seems plausible, but it definitely highlighted how much we're all trying to make sense of a very quiet start. I'm just still struggling to find anyone sticking out, which is frustrating.
